step.text: Minimum 10 individual producers or 2 producer institutions
step.name: Step 1

step.text: Hold first AGM and appoint directors
step.name: Step 6

acceptedAnswer.text: 10 individual producers OR 2 producer institutions (cooperative societies, producer companies).

mainEntity.name: Can a producer company distribute profits?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes, through patronage bonus based on volume of business done by each member with the company. Standard dividend distribution also permitted.

Company of producers (farmers, artisans, craftsmen) formed under Part IXA of Companies Act, 1956 (retained). Objects: production, harvesting, procurement, grading, pooling, handling, marketing, selling, export of primary produce.
Minimum 10 individual producers OR 2 producer institutions (cooperatives, societies). No maximum limit. Only producers can be members — not traders or processors.

mainEntity.name: What are the benefits of producer company?

acceptedAnswer.text: Limited liability (unlike cooperatives). Better governance (Companies Act compliance). Easier to raise credit. Tax benefits for agricultural income. Government scheme eligibility. FPO (Farmer Producer Organisation) status.

mainEntity.name: Is a producer company eligible for government subsidies?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes. NABARD and state government schemes for FPOs. Equity grant up to ₹15 lakhs under SFAC scheme. Priority lending from banks. APMC bypass for direct marketing.

mainEntity.name: What compliance is needed for producer company?

acceptedAnswer.text: Annual filing (AOC-4, MGT-7), AGM within 6 months, board meetings (min 4/year), accounts audit if turnover exceeds threshold. Lighter compliance than regular companies for certain provisions.
10 individual producers OR 2 producer institutions (cooperative societies, producer companies).
